qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Bulk download, upload, and delete helpers bypass `.goshs` folder ACLs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding identifies the same missing `.goshs` ACL enforcement on `PUT`, `POST .../upload`, and `?delete` routes that the advisory fixes in `httpserver/updown.go` and `httpserver/handler.go`. Its extra `?bulk` read bypass is broader than the advisory but does not change the shared root cause on the advisory-matched mutation paths.

selected_finding.title: Bulk download, upload, and delete helpers bypass `.goshs` folder ACLs

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches GHSA-wvhv-qcqf-f3cx because it identifies the same missing `.goshs` authorization checks on state-changing file-server routes. In the copied PoC workspace, an unauthenticated requester successfully wrote into a protected folder with `PUT`, deleted `protected/.goshs` through the vulnerable delete route, and then read `protected/secret.txt` without credentials.
evidence: The advisory fix commit `f212c4f4a126556bab008f79758e21a839ef2c0f` adds `.goshs` auth enforcement to `put()`, `upload()`, `deleteFile()`, and `handleMkdir()`, and blocks deletion or overwrite of `.goshs` itself.

The selected finding report points to the same vulnerable code family: `httpserver/server.go` dispatches `POST .../upload` and `PUT`, `httpserver/updown.go` performs the writes, and `httpserver/handler.go` handles `?delete` without reloading and enforcing the folder ACL.

Fresh runtime evidence from `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_8cd3143b/.judge/poc_runs/http_acl_non_get_bypass/run.log` shows `/protected/` and `/protected/secret.txt` were initially blocked, unauthenticated `PUT` inside the protected folder succeeded, unauthenticated deletion of `protected/.goshs` succeeded, and the previously protected `secret.txt` then became readable.

The copied verifier emitted `EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS: unauthenticated PUT and delete bypassed protected .goshs ACL and exposed secret.txt`, satisfying both bug activation and exploit-success runtime predicates.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied verifier confirmed that `/protected/` and `/protected/secret.txt` initially required authentication, then an unauthenticated `PUT` created `put-created.txt` inside the protected directory, an unauthenticated `?delete` removed `protected/.goshs`, and a follow-up unauthenticated GET returned `200` with the contents of the previously protected `secret.txt`.
